meaning: An advertisement designed to look like a normal, objective news article or editorial column.
definition: An advertisement designed to look like a normal, objective news article or editorial column.
example: Although it looked like a health advice article, it was actually an advertorial promoting a new brand of vitamins.

meaning: A website or software tool that collects news stories from many different sources and shows them in one place.
definition: A website or software tool that collects news stories from many different sources and shows them in one place.
example: He uses a news aggregator app to see stories from five different newspapers on his phone screen.

meaning: A person who writes and regularly updates an online journal or blog, expressing personal opinions or covering specific topics.
definition: A person who writes and regularly updates an online journal or blog, expressing personal opinions or covering specific topics.
example: The food blogger gained thousands of followers by writing honest reviews of local restaurants.

meaning: A large-format newspaper printed on large sheets of paper, known for serious, in-depth articles and international news.
definition: A large-format newspaper printed on large sheets of paper, known for serious, in-depth articles and international news.
example: She prefers reading a broadsheet because she finds its political analysis more reliable than tabloid stories.

meaning: The average number of copies of a newspaper or magazine that are distributed or sold each day or week.
example: Due to the rise of free online articles, the physical circulation of the local newspaper has dropped significantly.

meaning: News reporting and analysis conducted by ordinary citizens rather than professional journalists, often using social media.
definition: News reporting and analysis conducted by ordinary citizens rather than professional journalists, often using social media.
example: The video recorded by a bystander during the protest was a powerful example of citizen journalism.

meaning: Small advertisements in a newspaper or website, organized by categories, where people buy, sell, or rent items and advertise jobs.
definition: Small advertisements in a newspaper or website, organized by categories, where people buy, sell, or rent items and advertise jobs.
example: He looked through the classified ads in the local paper to find a cheap used bicycle.

meaning: A brief statement or symbol containing details about the publication's printing, paper type, and typography, usually found at the back.
definition: A brief statement or symbol containing details about the publication's printing, paper type, and typography, usually found at the back.
example: The designer examined the colophon of the art magazine to find out which font had been used for the titles.

meaning: An online version of a printed newspaper or magazine that can be read on computers, tablets, or smartphones.
definition: An online version of a printed newspaper or magazine that can be read on computers, tablets, or smartphones.
example: He cancelled his physical paper subscription and signed up for the digital edition to read on his tablet.

meaning: False or misleading stories presented as real news, often spread online to influence people's opinions or make money.
definition: False or misleading stories presented as real news, often spread online to influence people's opinions or make money.
example: Social media platforms are trying to block the spread of fake news during election campaigns.

meaning: The very first page of a newspaper, where the most important and urgent news of the day is displayed.
definition: The very first page of a newspaper, where the most important and urgent news of the day is displayed.
example: The signing of the peace treaty was featured on the front page of every major newspaper.

meaning: A serious academic or professional publication containing scholarly articles written by experts in a specific field.
definition: A serious academic or professional publication containing scholarly articles written by experts in a specific field.
example: The doctor published his research findings in a respected medical journal.

meaning: The way in which text, headlines, and photographs are arranged on a newspaper page or a screen.
definition: The way in which text, headlines, and photographs are arranged on a newspaper page or a screen.
example: The layout designer decided to place the dramatic photo right next to the main headline for maximum impact.

meaning: The printed section of a newspaper or magazine, usually on the editorial page, listing the publication's name, staff, and owners.
definition: The printed section of a newspaper or magazine, usually on the editorial page, listing the publication's name, staff, and owners.
example: If you want to know who the editor-in-chief of the magazine is, you can check the masthead.

meaning: A small stall or outdoor booth where newspapers, magazines, and maps are sold to the public.
definition: A small stall or outdoor booth where newspapers, magazines, and maps are sold to the public.
example: She stopped at a newsstand near the subway station to buy a morning paper and a bottle of water.

meaning: An article in a newspaper that announces a person's death and gives a brief summary of their life and accomplishments.
definition: An article in a newspaper that announces a person's death and gives a brief summary of their life and accomplishments.
example: She wrote a beautiful obituary for her grandfather, highlighting his years of service as a local doctor.

meaning: A system on a website that requires visitors to pay a subscription fee to access articles and premium content.
definition: A system on a website that requires visitors to pay a subscription fee to access articles and premium content.
example: I wanted to read the full report on the website, but I ran into a paywall asking for a monthly subscription.

meaning: A magazine or journal that is published at regular intervals, such as weekly, monthly, or quarterly.
definition: A magazine or journal that is published at regular intervals, such as weekly, monthly, or quarterly.
example: The library has a special section dedicated to periodicals, including science journals and design magazines.

meaning: A package of promotional materials, photos, and information sent to journalists to help them write about a company or product.
definition: A package of promotional materials, photos, and information sent to journalists to help them write about a company or product.
example: The movie studio sent a detailed press kit to movie critics before the official premiere.

meaning: A machine that applies ink to paper to mass-produce newspapers, books, and magazines.
definition: A machine that applies ink to paper to mass-produce newspapers, books, and magazines.
example: The invention of the printing press in the fifteenth century made books affordable for ordinary people.

meaning: An agreement to pay a regular amount of money to receive issues of a publication or access to a website.
example: She bought a yearly subscription to the science magazine so she wouldn't miss an issue.

meaning: An article or column written by a popular writer that is sold to and printed by many different newspapers at the same time.
definition: An article or column written by a popular writer that is sold to and printed by many different newspapers at the same time.
example: Her syndicated column about family life appears in over fifty newspapers across the country every Wednesday.

meaning: A small-sized newspaper that focuses on sensational news, celebrity gossip, crime, and has lots of large photos.
definition: A small-sized newspaper that focuses on sensational news, celebrity gossip, crime, and has lots of large photos.
example: He bought a tabloid at the supermarket to read about the latest rumors surrounding the royal family.

meaning: Any form of content, such as videos, blogs, or comments, created and posted online by everyday users of a platform.
definition: Any form of content, such as videos, blogs, or comments, created and posted online by everyday users of a platform.
example: Many modern news sites include user-generated content, like video clips sent in by readers during breaking events.
